title: Semantik Architect Frame: Relational membership / affiliation
description: Canonical semantic frame for group membership and affiliation relations between an entity and a group, organization, party, team, institution, or similar collective. This schema preserves legacy field compatibility while normalizing toward the current relational-family contracts.

frame_type.description: Frame type identifier. New data should prefer `relation.membership`. Legacy aliases remain accepted during migration.

frame_family.description: Optional high-level frame family label. For this schema it is always relational.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for this frame instance, such as a statement cluster ID or upstream assertion ID.

member.description: The entity that belongs to or is affiliated with the group.

group.description: The collective, organization, party, club, institution, movement, or team to which the member belongs.

relation_type.description: Coarse label describing the membership relation, e.g. `member`, `affiliate`, `employee`, `player`, `supporter`, `fellow`, `alumnus`.

role_label.description: Optional more specific role or function within the group, e.g. `goalkeeper`, `chairperson`, `research_fellow`, `captain`.

time_span.description: Preferred canonical time span during which the membership or affiliation holds.

start.description: Legacy compatibility field for the beginning of the membership relation. Prefer `time_span` for new data.

end.description: Legacy compatibility field for the end of the membership relation. Prefer `time_span` for new data.

status.description: Optional status label such as `current`, `former`, `honorary`, `acting`, `suspended`, or `inactive`.

certainty.description: Optional confidence or certainty marker for the relation.

source_id.description: Optional identifier for the source record from which this relation was derived.

source_entities.description: Optional source or asserting entities relevant to the relation, such as the organization itself, a registry body, or an authoritative dataset publisher.

attributes.description: Free-form structured attributes for membership-specific details not yet promoted to top-level fields, such as `membership_number`, `team_number`, `portfolio`, `electoral_list_position`, or `selection_method`.

extra.description: Free-form metadata preserved for provenance, normalization traces, raw-source values, or debugging.

EntityRef.description: Lightweight reference to an entity used inside relational frames. At least one stable identifier or label should be present.

id.description: Opaque internal identifier for the entity.

entity_id.description: Canonical or planner-stable entity identifier when different from `id`.

qid.description: Optional Wikidata QID or similar external knowledge-base identifier.

name.description: Preferred human-readable label for the entity.

label.description: Optional alternative surface label. Kept for compatibility with slot-map style entity objects.

surface_key.description: Optional planner-stable discourse key for this entity reference.

entity_type.description: Optional coarse type hint such as `person`, `organization`, `party`, `team`, `institution`, or `movement`.

kind.description: Optional looser kind label retained for compatibility with older schema shapes.

gender.description: Optional coarse gender category for human entities.

human.description: Whether the referenced entity is human.

lemmas.description: Optional associated lemma strings useful during lexical resolution or realization.

features.description: Arbitrary structured features used by normalization, lexical resolution, or morphology.

extra.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the entity reference.

TimeSpan.description: Coarse or exact temporal span. Supports both the repo's year/month/day compatibility shape and a more explicit start/end/point string form.

start.description: Optional interval start in ISO-8601-like string form.

end.description: Optional interval end in ISO-8601-like string form.

point.description: Optional single time point in ISO-8601-like string form.

precision.description: Optional precision hint such as `year`, `month`, `day`, or `approximate`.

approximate.description: Whether the time span is approximate.
